rationale: Educ. Code §22.052 governs school medication administration and liability immunity. It has no operative connection to Tax Code §11.13(b) homestead exemption amounts. No conflict exists.
quote: On the adoption of policies concerning the administration of medication to students by school district, open-enrollment charter school, or private school employees, the school district, open-enrollment charter school, or private school, its board of trustees or other governing body, as applicable, and its employees are immune from civil liability and administrative disciplinary action for damages or injuries resulting from the administration of medication to a student
_note: Rule text is entirely unrelated to the proposed homestead exemption change; no overlap in subject matter.
